steps = min(max(steps_per_epoch * WARMUP_EPOCHS, 1), max(total_steps // 10, 1))\\n\\n    def lr_lambda(step: int) -> float:\\n        if step < warmup_steps:\\n            return float(step + 1) / float(warmup_steps)\\n        progress = (step - warmup_steps) / max(total_steps - warmup_steps, 1)\\n        return 0.05 + 0.95 * 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))\\n\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lr_lambda=lr_lambda)\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"With only 256 optimizer steps, the baseline underuses the fixed-step budget.
